message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should return base premium for a single component"
Expected behavior: generateQuote([{type: "rune", ...}], customer) returns object with basePremium of 25
Current error: Expected 25, Received undefined (rune not in BASE_PREMIUMS map)
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Note: Components (runes, moonstones, etc.) each have a base premium of 25 G. They are treated as a separate "component" category.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: Let me read the current state of both files.

The failing test uses `type: "rune"` which is not in `BASE_PREMIUMS`. The minimal fix is to add "rune" (and the component category) with a value of 25 to the map.

Per the note, components (runes, moonstones, etc.) all have a base premium of 25 G. The minimal implementation is simply adding an entry for "rune" to the `BASE_PREMIUMS` map — that is all the current test demands.

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_07-09-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/claim-office.ts has been updated successfully.

content.text: Now run the tests to verify.

content.text:
All 5 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added `rune: 25` to the `BASE_PREMIUMS` map in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_07-09-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/claim-office.ts`

**Result**: All tests now pass (5 passing, 21 todo)

**Approach**: The single failing test looked up `items[0].type` ("rune") in `BASE_PREMIUMS` and got `undefined`. The minimal fix is one new key-value pair in the existing map — no new logic, no generalisation of "component" category, no handling of other component types like "moonstone" that are not yet demanded by any test.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
